message.content:
You are an agent in a multi-agent MoE (Mixture of Experts) pipeline. Your job: enrich LEAD-0116 Hunter St. Hospitality.

Context:
- Lead: LEAD-0116, Hunter St. Hospitality
- Website: https://huntersthospitality.com.au/
- Known KP: Frank Tucker, CEO
- Problem: KP name is known but no direct email/phone found
- Country: Australia

Your task:
1. Fetch the company website homepage and look for contact info
2. Check their /about, /team, /contact pages for Frank Tucker's direct contact
3. Look for any email addresses (especially person emails like firstname.lastname@domain)
4. Look for phone numbers (Australian format: 04xx mobile or 02/03/07/08 landline)
5. Look for LinkedIn URLs for Frank Tucker

Report back with:
- Any emails found (classify as person/department/company)
- Any phones found (classify as mobile/landline/company)
- Any LinkedIn URLs found
- Source URL for each finding
- Confidence level (High/Medium/Low)

Use WebFetch to check pages. Start with the team/culinary page at: https://huntersthospitality.com.au/culinary-costodians/

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init
- review
- security-review

content.text:
All external access tools (WebFetch, Bash, WebSearch) are denied in this session. I can only work with existing project data. Here is my enrichment report based on a thorough review of all existing data.

---

## LEAD-0116 Hunter St. Hospitality -- Enrichment Report

**Status:** BLOCKED -- cannot fetch live web pages. All findings below are from prior pipeline runs.

### Existing Data Summary

**Lead record** (`data/leads.csv` line 117):
- Website: https://huntersthospitality.com.au/
- Contact page: https://huntersthospitality.com.au/contact-us/
- Company LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/hunter-st-hospitality/
- Contact status: 已找到联系路径 (contact path found via contact form)
- KP: Frank Tucker, CEO
- KP source page: https://huntersthospitality.com.au/hunter-st-hospitality-ceo/

**Contacts in** `data/contacts.csv` (3 entries for LEAD-0116):

| ID | Name | Title | Email | Phone | LinkedIn | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CONTACT-0012 | Frank Tucker | CEO | -- | -- | https://www.linkedin.com/in/frank-tucker-3a0796165 | 已找到直联 |
| CONTACT-0013 | Santiago Aristizabal | Culinary Custodian | -- | -- | LinkedIn search URL (待人工确认) | 已识别联系人 |
| CONTACT-0014 | Andy Evans | Executive Chef, Spice Temple | -- | -- | LinkedIn search URL (待人工确认) | 已识别联系人 |

### What Was Already Searched (prior pipeline runs)

Multiple KP pipeline runs (2026-05-19) and extraction batches (2026-05-12/13) searched this lead. Findings:
- **Contact form**: https://huntersthospitality.com.au/contact-us/ (score 102)
- **Company LinkedIn**: https://www.linkedin.com/company/hunter-st-hospitality/ (score 70)
- **Frank Tucker LinkedIn**: https://www.linkedin.com/in/frank-tucker-3a0796165 (confirmed via Bing search)
- **No personal email found** for any KP
- **No phone number found** (no mobile 04xx or landline 02/03/07/08)
- **No person-level email** (no firstname.lastname@ pattern)
- One extraction attempt got an SSLError on the site (2026-05-13)

### Classification of Existing Findings

| Finding | Type | Classification | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Frank Tucker LinkedIn URL | LinkedIn personal profile | High value | Medium | Bing search (prior run) |
| Contact form URL | Company contact form | Low value | High | Official website |
| Company LinkedIn URL | Company page | Low value | Medium | Official website |
| Santiago Aristizabal LinkedIn search URL | LinkedIn search (unverified) | Low value | Low | Generated URL |
| Andy Evans LinkedIn search URL | LinkedIn search (unverified) | Low value | Low | Generated URL |

### Gaps -- What Still Needs to Be Found

1. **Frank Tucker direct email** -- No firstname.lastname@ or similar pattern found. The company appears not to publish personal emails on their website.
2. **Frank Tucker phone** -- No mobile (04xx) or landline found.
3. **Santiago Aristizabal verified LinkedIn** -- Only a search URL exists, needs manual verification.
4. **Andy Evans verified LinkedIn** -- Only a search URL exists, needs manual verification.

### Recommendation

Per Rule 3 (KP Search Investment Limits), this lead has been searched across multiple pipeline runs without finding direct email or phone. The LinkedIn profile for Frank Tucker is confirmed. Without web access in this session, I cannot perform additional scraping.

**Next steps that require tool permissions:**
1. Use WebFetch or curl to fetch https://huntersthospitality.com.au/contact-us/ and extract any email/phone
2. Use WebFetch or curl to fetch https://huntersthospitality.com.au/hunter-st-hospitality-ceo/ for direct contact details
3. Use WebSearch to search for "frank.tucker@huntersthospitality.com.au" or similar patterns
4. Check if the domain has a standard email format (e.g., firstname@huntersthospitality.com.au)

**Current enrichment result:** No new data found. This session is blocked from external access. The lead already has Frank Tucker's LinkedIn profile and contact form URL. Direct email/phone remains undiscovered after multiple prior attempts, suggesting the company does not publicly list personal contact details for leadership.
